prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `6350f1f4`.
codex_response:
The rule is: the input is an `n x n` array of `n x n` tiles separated by single-cell `0` bands, with `5` acting as corruption. A clean mixed two-color tile is the reference motif; in the output, every tile that still contains the reference majority color becomes a solid majority tile, every minority-only tile becomes the clean reference motif, and all separator noise is cleared to `0`.

I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint, and discarded the older sonnet-style “position-wise consensus” interpretation; that mismatch is documented in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_6350f1f4/notes.md#L1). No new shared primitive was needed. The generator matches the family by sampling square `n x n` layouts, a majority/minority reference motif, minority-only outlier tiles, and sparse `5` corruption both inside tiles and on separator rows/columns while preserving at least one clean reference tile.

Verification passed: official examples `5/5`, generated examples `1000/1000`, and the built preview sheet looked on-distribution.
